preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 79.5% to win, with Jordan's chances at just 3.9%. The underdog faces a steep climb, though the inclusion of a draw probability—16.6%—suggests the match retains some competitive uncertainty rather than being a complete mismatch.

The win-loss gap is substantial, but the draw probability is neither trivial nor dominant. Argentina is heavily favored on the three-way spread, yet draws remain plausible at roughly one in six outcomes. The asymmetry reflects a strong favorite facing a weaker opponent on a neutral ground, without the dynamic collapsing into a near-certain result.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned. The market places Argentina at 78.8%, just 0.7 percentage points below the model; Jordan sits at 7.0% versus 3.9%, a gap of 3.1pp. Polymarket prices show slightly wider confidence in Argentina at 81.3%. These small divergences fall within typical variance and suggest broad agreement that Argentina enters as the clear favorite.
